Politics.headline: Third House resignation in 8 days as Cherfilus-McCormick steps down
Politics.detail: Representative Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick resigned from Congress just before the House Ethics Committee was set to recommend a punishment for her, likely on the cusp of being expelled due to an ethics investigation into her use of campaign funds.

Politics.headline: Two CIA members killed in car crash after Mexico drug raid, report says
Politics.detail: Two CIA agents were reportedly killed in a car crash in the Mexican state of Chihuahua following a drug raid. President Trump has been putting pressure on Mexico to act against cartels, leading to broadened CIA anti-drug operations in the country.

Politics.headline: Trump extends Iran ceasefire, citing 'fractured' Iranian government
Politics.detail: President Trump extended the Iran ceasefire, pointing to a 'fractured' Iranian government, though uncertainty remains regarding US-Iran peace talks. Since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes against Iran on Feb. 28, Trump has made contradictory statements about ending the war.

Politics.headline: Southern Poverty Law Center indicted on federal fraud charges
Politics.detail: The Southern Poverty Law Center was indicted on federal fraud charges for allegedly improperly raising millions of dollars to pay informants to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan and other extremist groups. Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche announced the charges.

Politics.headline: Appeals court rules Texas can require public schools to display Ten Commandments in class
Politics.detail: An appeals court ruled that Texas can require public schools to display the Ten Commandments in classrooms. This ruling comes amid ongoing debates about the role of religion in public education.

Finance & Economy.headline: Stocks slip and oil prices rise on uncertainty about US-Iran ceasefire talks
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. stocks and oil prices flip-flopped as uncertainty rose about what will happen following a two-week ceasefire in the war with Iran, which had been set to expire Wednesday.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump's pick for Fed chair heads to Capitol Hill
Finance & Economy.detail: Kevin Warsh, nominated by President Trump to lead the Federal Reserve, is scheduled to appear before the Senate Banking Committee today.

Finance & Economy.headline: News sites are blocking access to Internet Archive's Wayback Machine
Finance & Economy.detail: Several news organizations are now blocking the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine from archiving their content, raising concerns about access to historical information.

Finance & Economy.headline: Primark to split from food business despite warning of Iran war impact
Finance & Economy.detail: Primark is reportedly planning to separate from its food business operations despite warnings about the potential economic impact of the Iran war.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK jobs market was in a fragile state – even before Iran war threatened recovery
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK jobs market was showing signs of weakness prior to the escalation of the Iran war, casting further doubt on the country's economic recovery.

Technology.headline: Tim Cook to step down as Apple chief as John Ternus named replacement
Technology.detail: Apple's CEO Tim Cook will step down to become executive chairman, with senior vice president of hardware engineering John Ternus taking over as CEO effective September 1. Ternus has spent 25 years with Apple, overseeing the engineering of the iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

Technology.headline: Most serious cyberattacks against the UK now from Russia, Iran and China, cyber chief will say
Technology.detail: The head of the U.K.'s National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) will announce that the most serious cyberattacks in the U.K. are now carried out by hostile nations including Russia, Iran and China. Criminal activity like ransomware remains common, but state-sponsored attacks pose the gravest threat.

Technology.headline: Trilateral research advances aircraft materials testing
Technology.detail: Australia, Japan, and the United States have formed a trilateral Defence Science and Technology agreement focusing on virtual structural testing for composite aircraft materials. The project aims to improve understanding of damage initiation and propagation in composite materials used in modern military aircraft, enhancing the accuracy and reliability of predictive tools.

Technology.headline: Magnachip Launches 8th-generation Ultra Low-Rss(on) 12V BatteryFET Designed for Smartphone Battery Power Efficiency
Technology.detail: Magnachip Semiconductor Corporation has launched new 8th-generation Ultra Low-Ron 12V low-voltage MOSFETs for high-performance smartphone battery protection circuits, targeting next-generation smartphones with ultra-fast charging and energy efficiency needs. The new products reduce specific on-resistance by approximately 48% and improve current density by approximately 185% compared to the previous generation.

Technology.headline: Tech sector to improve on pricing, AI demand
Technology.detail: HLIB Research indicates that improvements in price adjustments and increased exposure to AI data centers are boosting the local technology sector. Channel checks suggest selected outsourced semiconductor assembly and test (Osat) companies are engaging customers on potential price increases.

Science.headline: Trilateral research advances aircraft materials testing
Science.detail: Australia, Japan, and the United States have formed a Defence Science and Technology agreement to enhance the testing and certification of advanced composite aircraft materials, focusing on damage understanding in modern military aircraft. The three nations will share experimental test data and compare modeling outcomes to improve specialized software developed in the US.

Science.headline: World's leading science competition announces 25 international pioneers who can help humanity live within Earth's boundaries
Science.detail: The Frontiers Planet Prize announced 25 National Champions for their science-based solutions to the planetary crisis, addressing challenges like climate change, biodiversity loss, and economic governance. These champions are providing insights for a new report created in partnership with the United Nations University Centre for Policy Research (UNU-CPR) and the Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research.

Science.headline: Scientists Just Captured Trees Glowing with Electricity During Storms
Science.detail: Scientists chasing thunderstorms in a retrofitted minivan captured faint electrical glows shimmering from treetops during a storm, known as “corona discharges,” which were long suspected but never observed before.

Science.headline: New Study Links Coffee Intake to Microbiome Changes and Improved Mental Well-Being
Science.detail: A new study from University College Cork suggests that both caffeinated and decaf coffee can alter the gut microbiome, leading to reduced stress and improved mental well-being, offering insights into the drink's potential health benefits. The research indicates that coffee has previously unrecognized effects on the microbiota-gut-brain axis.

Science.headline: B.C. Ferries alters Northern Expedition schedule amid efforts to avoid hitting humpback whales
Science.detail: BC Ferries is changing its Northern Expedition schedule to avoid hitting humpback whales. Scientists have long known that deepsea female anglerfish use their glowing lures to draw in unwitting prey.

Health.headline: AAN 2026: 'Remarkable' Early Results for Novel Immune Therapy in SPS
Health.detail: Medscape Medical News reports on early results of a novel immune therapy for Stiff-Person Syndrome (SPS) presented at the American Academy of Neurology (AAN) meeting.

Health.headline: Eating Avocado and Mango Daily May Improve Blood Pressure in Prediabetes
Health.detail: A recent study found that eating one avocado and 1 cup of mango daily may help lower blood pressure and improve heart health in adults with prediabetes.

Health.headline: U.S. to Ask States to Revalidate "high-risk" Medicaid Providers
Health.detail: Reuters Health Information reports that the U.S. government will ask states to revalidate "high-risk" Medicaid providers.

Health.headline: Deadly domestic violence cases stir calls for more prevention resources for Black communities
Health.detail: Increased domestic violence cases are prompting calls for more prevention resources for Black communities.

Health.headline: Why Constipation Remains Underrecognized in Pregnancy
Health.detail: Medscape News Europe discusses why constipation continues to be underrecognized during pregnancy.

Sports.headline: SRH vs DC IPL 2026: Eshan Malinga's 4 wickets, Abhishek Sharma's 135 help Hyderabad defeat Delhi by 47 runs
Sports.detail: Eshan Malinga took 3 wickets while Abhishek Sharma slammed an unbeaten 135 as Sunrisers Hyderabad defeated the Delhi Capitals by 47 runs in the IPL 2026 match.

Sports.headline: Brighton hand Chelsea fifth straight PL defeat to pile pressure on Rosenior
Sports.detail: Brighton defeated Chelsea, increasing the pressure on Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior, who labeled Chelsea's performance 'unacceptable'.

Sports.headline: MS Dhoni edges closer to IPL 2026 return after wicketkeeping stint at Wankhede Stadium
Sports.detail: MS Dhoni has been recovering from a calf strain and has missed CSK's first six matches; the franchise released a statement that he would miss the first two weeks of the tournament, potentially edging closer to an IPL 2026 return.

Sports.headline: ‘The ocean gods helped us out’: Oar Blacks ride the ocean to landmark first win
Sports.detail: The Oar Blacks women became the first Kiwi crew to beat Australia in a landmark victory.

Sports.headline: O'Sullivan overcomes Crucible misstep to lead World Championship opener
Sports.detail: Ronnie O'Sullivan overcame a misstep at the Crucible to lead the World Championship opener.

Entertainment.headline: Bhooth Bangla Box Office Collection Day 5: Akshay Kumar's film shows growth, crosses Rs 72 crore on Tuesday
Entertainment.detail: Akshay Kumar's horror comedy, 'Bhooth Bangla', continues to perform well at the box office, earning Rs 7.65 crore on Tuesday, bringing its total collection in India to Rs 72.4 crore. The film has grossed Rs 117.71 crore worldwide.

Entertainment.headline: D4vd Murder Case: Celebrated New Music Days After Allegedly Killing Celeste
Entertainment.detail: Singer D4vd is facing murder charges in connection to the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas, and new details have emerged about him celebrating new music releases shortly after the alleged crime. He has pleaded not guilty.

Entertainment.headline: Kim Jae-hwan's 'competitive fire' softens into a comforting flame in new digital single
Entertainment.detail: Kim Jae-hwan, formerly of Wanna One, is releasing a new digital single, "I'll Be There," which he says reflects his desire to provide comfort through his music rather than focusing on competition. Kim participated in composing and writing the song, playing the guitar to bring warmth to the music.

Entertainment.headline: Deepika Padukone, Ranveer Singh announce second pregnancy
Entertainment.detail: Bollywood stars Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh have announced that they are expecting their second child. The announcement was made via a social media post.

Entertainment.headline: Madonna offers reward after vintage Coachella costume goes missing
Entertainment.detail: Madonna is offering a reward after a vintage costume she wore during her Coachella performance went missing. The details of the costume and the reward have not been disclosed.

Crime & Justice.headline: Southern Poverty Law Center charged with defrauding donors with payments to extremist informants
Crime & Justice.detail: The Southern Poverty Law Center was indicted Tuesday on federal fraud charges alleging it improperly raised millions of dollars to pay informants to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan and other extremist groups.

Crime & Justice.headline: Walsall rapist changes pleas to guilty after attack on Sikh woman he thought was Muslim
Crime & Justice.detail: John Ashby changed his pleas to guilty of rape, intentional strangulation, robbery and racially-aggravated assault after attacking a Sikh woman in Walsall, wrongly believing she was Muslim. He will be sentenced on April 24th.

Crime & Justice.headline: Federal Grand Jury Charges Southern Poverty Law Center for Wire Fraud, False Statements, and Conspiracy to Commit Money Laundering
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department announced that a federal grand jury has indicted the Southern Poverty Law Center on charges of wire fraud, false statements, and conspiracy to commit money laundering.

Crime & Justice.headline: 'Ketchup modus' nearly targets student inside the jeepney in Laguna
Crime & Justice.detail: A student in San Pedro City, Laguna, narrowly avoided being a victim of the 'ketchup modus,' a distraction theft scheme where suspects spill a substance on a victim and then attempt to steal their belongings. The student stayed alert and exited the vehicle before any theft could occur.

Crime & Justice.headline: Meth-soaked clothing in traveller's suitcase at Auckland Airport
Crime & Justice.detail: A traveller who arrived at Auckland International Airport from Vancouver appeared in court after 26 items of clothing in his suitcase were found to be soaked in methamphetamine. Customs officers discovered the drugs during a baggage search.

Education.headline: Texas can require public schools to display Ten Commandments in classrooms, US appeals court rules
Education.detail: A federal appeals court rules that Texas can require the Ten Commandments to be displayed in public school classrooms, allowing posters of the religious doctrine to go up throughout the state.

Education.headline: Schools get another year to comply with web accessibility deadlines
Education.detail: Schools now have an additional year to meet web accessibility requirements.

Education.headline: LAUSD to vote on restricting student screen time, after years of encouraging classroom use
Education.detail: After years of promoting screen time in the classroom, the Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) is set to vote on measures that would restrict student screen time.

Education.headline: Teaching Profession 2026 Teacher of the Year Preps History Students for a Diverse and Divisive World
Education.detail: Leon Smith, the 2026 Teacher of the Year, prepares his history students for navigating a diverse and divisive world.

Education.headline: Community colleges racing to train L.A. construction workers
Education.detail: Community colleges in Los Angeles are actively working to train construction workers.

Lifestyle.headline: Chen Xi and wife host Japan wedding ceremony with guests from across the globe
Lifestyle.detail: Singaporean actor-artist Chen Xi and his wife held their wedding at a Japanese Shinto Munakata Taisha shrine with friends and family from 12 different countries in attendance.

Lifestyle.headline: Lessons from TED 2026 on Technology and the Future of Leadership
Lifestyle.detail: TED 2026 discussed topics ranging from AI and consciousness to environmental progress and public health crises, featuring speakers like Malala Yousafzai and Bill McKibben.

Lifestyle.headline: Drew McCartor of Pure Earth spoke at TED 2026 about lead poisoning
Lifestyle.detail: Drew McCartor, co-CEO of Pure Earth, discussed lead poisoning as a significant, yet often overlooked, global public health crisis affecting one in three children globally. Curbing lead pollution is a straightforward way to improve cognitive development.

Lifestyle.headline: Entertainment & Lifestyle from Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Midlands 103 provides a listing of their entertainment and lifestyle programming for Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026. Currently, there are no stories to show.

Lifestyle.headline: Bear Grylls reveals why A-list stars willingly eat snakes, brave glaciers on 'Running Wild'
Lifestyle.detail: Bear Grylls discusses the appeal of his show 'Running Wild' where celebrities participate in extreme outdoor adventures.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Augean stables called DPWH
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece discusses the state of the Department of Public Works and Highways in the Philippines.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Someone has to build the floor first
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This is a personal essay reflecting on the challenges and perspectives of navigating university life with financial struggles and societal expectations. It highlights the invisible burdens and pressures faced by those striving for a better future.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — April 22, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article includes letters to the editor regarding the defeat of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, which linked women's reservation to a new delimitation exercise in the Lok Sabha. It also highlights the opposition to redrawing electoral maps and emphasizes that true women's empowerment rests on social justice.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A debate over performance bonuses
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the internal tensions at Samsung Electronics over performance bonuses amid a semiconductor boom, contrasting it with the company's bleak outlook just three years ago. It also mentions rival SK hynix and labor unions seeking their share, and the absence of discussions about research and development or capital investment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Inflation matters more than growth
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial argues that inflation is a more pressing concern than economic growth for Taiwanese citizens, especially with high global crude oil prices and the ongoing war in the Middle East. It notes that while Taiwan's economy is expected to grow, rising living expenses are a more immediate issue for the population.

World.headline: Trump Announces Extension of Iran Ceasefire, Blockade Remains
World.detail: President Trump has announced an extension to the ceasefire with Iran, but stated that the U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will remain in effect. This announcement comes amidst ongoing uncertainty surrounding peace talks between the U.S. and Iran.

World.headline: Nearly 560,000 Sudanese Refugees Enter Libya Amid Sudan Conflict: UN report
World.detail: A UN report indicates that over 559,920 Sudanese refugees have entered Libya since the conflict in Sudan began in April 2023, creating one of the largest displacement crises in the region. Tightened border measures by Libyan authorities are forcing refugees to take more dangerous routes.

World.headline: Former NASA Astronauts Launch Nonprofit Focused on Civic Responsibility
World.detail: Over 100 former NASA astronauts have launched a nonpartisan nonprofit called Astronauts For America to promote civic responsibility and address concerns about democratic governance in the United States. The organization, independent of NASA, plans to evaluate political candidates and engage with policymakers.

World.headline: Renewables Overtake Power Demand
World.detail: A new global power analysis reveals that clean power generation, driven by record growth in solar energy in China and India, increased by 887 terawatt-hours (TWh) last year, exceeding the global electricity demand growth of 849 TWh. For the first time in modern history, renewables hit more than one-third of the world's electricity mix.

World.headline: Lao-China Power Grid Promotes Clean Energy
World.detail: The Laos-China 500 kV transmission line has commenced operations, aiming to boost cross-border electricity trade and promote clean energy growth. This initiative enhances power exchange capacity from 50 megawatts to 1,500 megawatts.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Celebrates 115 Days Without Load Shedding As Mixed Reactions Emerge
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has reached 115 days without load shedding, sparking mixed reactions from the public regarding the reliability and sustainability of the achievement. Some express optimism, while others remain skeptical.

Zimbabwe.headline: Report Anyone Transporting Explosives In Public Vehicles: ZRP Warns After Kombi Explosion
Zimbabwe.detail: Following a kombi explosion, the Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has urged the public to report anyone transporting explosives in public vehicles. This call aims to enhance public safety and prevent future incidents.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Government Seals Massive US$92.5 Million Victoria Falls Land Deal With JR Goddard Consortium
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwean government has finalized a significant US$92.5 million land deal in Victoria Falls with the JR Goddard Consortium. This deal is expected to boost development and investment in the region.

Zimbabwe.headline: Government convenes urgent talks to resolve nurses' strike
Zimbabwe.detail: The government has initiated urgent discussions to address the ongoing nurses' strike. The talks aim to find a resolution and ensure the resumption of normal healthcare services.

Zimbabwe.headline: Soldiers block High Court sheriff from evicting them from contested farm
Zimbabwe.detail: Soldiers have reportedly obstructed a High Court sheriff from carrying out an eviction on a disputed farm. The incident highlights ongoing land disputes and the involvement of the military.
